input.initialPrompt:
I want to display a photo gallery using CollectionView in a 3-column
grid layout with spacing between items. Each item should show an image
and a caption. How do I set this up in XAML?

data.content:
Here's a MAUI `CollectionView` with a 3-column grid layout:

```xml
<CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Photos}">
    <CollectionView.ItemsLayout>
        <GridItemsLayout Orientation="Vertical"
                         Span="3"
                         HorizontalItemSpacing="8"
                         VerticalItemSpacing="8" />
    </CollectionView.ItemsLayout>

    <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
        <DataTemplate>
            <VerticalStackLayout Padding="4">
                <Image Source="{Binding ImageSource}"
                       Aspect="AspectFill"
                       HeightRequest="120" />
                <Label Text="{Binding Caption}"
                       HorizontalTextAlignment="Center"
                       FontSize="12"
                       LineBreakMode="TailTruncation" />
            </VerticalStackLayout>
        </DataTemplate>
    </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
</CollectionView>
```

**Key points:**

- **`GridItemsLayout`** — `Span="3"` creates 3 columns; `Orientation="Vertical"` fills left-to-right, then wraps to the next row.
- **`HorizontalItemSpacing` / `VerticalItemSpacing`** — controls the gap between cells.
- **`DataTemplate`** — defines each cell's layout. Bind `ImageSource` and `Caption` to properties on your model.

Your backing model would look like:

```csharp
public class Photo
{
    public string ImageSource { get; set; }
    public string Caption { get; set; }
}
```
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about how to set up a .NET MAUI CollectionView in XAML with a 3-column grid layout, spacing, and items showing an image and caption. This is a general programming question - I can answer it directly from knowledge without needing to search the codebase.
